title: teesdalia lepidium
intro: Teesdalia lepidium, also known as the "woodland cress" or "bittercress", is a flowering plant species in the mustard family (Brassicaceae). It is commonly found in woodland areas and has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Teesdalia lepidium include its ability to aid digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Teesdalia lepidium can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's roots and leaves in tincture form.
